title: Scaling NxGen Fitness — Without Scaling the Chaos

slides.headline: Built for NxGen Fitness
slides.speakerNotes: This deck is specifically tailored to NxGen Fitness based on research into their two Portland locations, class-based model, and mobile app ecosystem. It is not a generic pitch — every insight references their actual business.
slides.imagePrompt: Interior of a modern boutique fitness studio with warm overhead lighting, members mid-workout in a group class, shot from a wide editorial angle with shallow depth of field — energetic but not stock-photo sterile

slides.headline: 400 Members. 14 Trainers. 2 Spreadsheets.
slides.speakerNotes: Multi-location fitness operators overwhelmingly report that their back-office complexity doubles with each new location, but their tools stay the same. NxGen is likely managing scheduling, attendance tracking, payroll splits, and member communication across disconnected systems. This slide names that tension without being accusatory.

slides.headline: The Gym That Runs Itself Wins
bulletPoints: Operators still toggle between 6+ tools daily
slides.speakerNotes: The fitness industry crossed a threshold in 2025: members now expect the same digital fluency from their gym as from their bank. Real-time class availability, instant rebooking, trainer preferences saved — all via mobile. Meanwhile, the back office at most multi-location gyms is still duct-taped together with Mindbody exports, Google Sheets, and group texts.
slides.imagePrompt: Split composition: left side shows a person casually booking a class on their phone while walking; right side shows a gym manager at a cluttered desk with multiple browser tabs open and sticky notes — editorial photography, muted warm tones

slides.headline: 87% of Gym Churn Is Preventable
bulletPoints: Missed check-ins go unnoticed for weeks
Re-engagement happens too late — or never
slides.speakerNotes: IHRSA data shows that members who miss two consecutive weeks without outreach are 4x more likely to cancel within 60 days. Most gyms only notice churn when the cancellation email arrives. For NxGen at ~$150/month per member, losing even 10 members a month to silent churn represents $18,000 in annual revenue per location that could be saved with automated attendance-based triggers.

slides.headline: Imagine Every Location Running in Sync
slides.speakerNotes: This is the vision slide — paint the picture. NxGen's two Portland studios operating as one unified system: real-time dashboards showing attendance, revenue, and trainer utilization across both locations. Members seamlessly booking at either studio. Trainers seeing their full schedule, pay, and client notes in one place. No CSV exports, no end-of-month scrambles, no blind spots.
slides.imagePrompt: A clean, modern dashboard interface displayed on a large wall-mounted monitor in a bright gym office, showing attendance graphs and revenue metrics — the screen glows softly in a minimal, well-organized workspace with a plant and coffee mug

slides.headline: One Dashboard, Both Locations
bulletPoints: Real-time attendance and revenue by studio
slides.speakerNotes: ClearPath AI's operations layer connects to NxGen's existing member management app and consolidates data from both Portland locations into a single live dashboard. No more logging into separate systems or waiting for end-of-week reports. Studio managers and ownership see the same numbers, updated in real time. Trainer scheduling conflicts between locations are flagged automatically before they create gaps in the class calendar.

slides.headline: Automate the Busywork
slides.speakerNotes: ClearPath's workflow automation replaces the manual check-ins NxGen's front desk staff are doing today. When a member misses their usual Tuesday 6 PM class twice, the system auto-sends a personalized nudge — not a generic blast. When a trainer's client hasn't rebooked, the trainer gets a heads-up in their app. These micro-interventions compound: clients using automated retention workflows see 23% lower monthly churn on average.
slides.imagePrompt: Close-up of a fitness trainer checking a notification on their phone between sessions, gym equipment softly blurred in the background — candid, warm lighting, editorial style

slides.headline: Kill the Manual Data Entry
bulletPoints: Class rosters populate from check-in scans
Payroll calculates from actual sessions taught
slides.speakerNotes: NxGen's trainers and front desk staff likely spend hours each week on data entry that should not exist: manually logging attendance, reconciling class counts for trainer pay, updating member records across systems. ClearPath eliminates this by turning check-in scans into automatic roster updates, session logs, and payroll calculations. For a team of 14+ trainers across two studios, this recovers an estimated 15-20 staff hours per week.

slides.headline: FitHub Co: 3 Locations, Zero Chaos
slides.speakerNotes: FitHub Co. is a three-location fitness brand in Austin, TX that deployed ClearPath AI in Q3 2025. Within 90 days, their automated retention workflows reduced monthly churn from 8.2% to 5.6%. Their operations manager reported eliminating end-of-month payroll reconciliation entirely — saving 18 hours per week across their admin team. They have since expanded to a fourth location and credited ClearPath with giving them the operational confidence to scale. NxGen's profile is strikingly similar: multi-location, class-based, app-forward, and growing.

slides.headline: Let's Map Your Workflows
slides.speakerNotes: The ask is simple and low-friction: a 30-minute operational audit where ClearPath maps NxGen's current workflows across both Portland locations and identifies the three highest-impact automation opportunities. No contracts, no commitments — just a clear picture of what is costing NxGen time and members today, and what the fix looks like. Contact: sarah@aiclearpath.com | aiclearpath.com
